output:
Implemented `src/shared/state/createEntityStore.ts` as the Zustand entity-store foundation for M1. The factory now creates one vanilla store per instantiation, keeps per-entity records under `entities[entityId]`, performs atomic bootstrap, lazy one-time loading, debounced persistence, text-field-only flush handling, clean-snapshot dirty tracking, and exposes the requested hook API plus test-facing helpers on `getState()` (`isDirty`, `hasPendingPersistence`, `syncExternalEntity`). Selector isolation is handled with `useStoreWithEqualityFn(..., shallow)` against the current entity slice.

Verification run for this batch:
- `npx tsc --noEmit`
- `npx vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/shared/state/createEntityStore.test.ts`

Checkpoint written to `.megaplan/plans/milestone-m1-from-docs-state-20260415-2100/execution_batch_2.json`.

task_updates.executor_notes: Implemented `src/shared/state/createEntityStore.ts` as a React Query-free Zustand factory with one vanilla store per instantiation, atomic bootstrap, lazy one-time entity loading, debounced persistence, text-field flush handling, clean-snapshot dirty tracking, test-facing helpers (`isDirty`, `hasPendingPersistence`, `syncExternalEntity`), and entity-slice subscription isolation via `useStoreWithEqualityFn(..., shallow)`. Verified with `npx tsc --noEmit` and the full `src/shared/state/createEntityStore.test.ts` module.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Yes. The factory stays React Query-free, owns one Zustand vanilla store per factory instantiation, exposes per-entity slices keyed by `entityId`, bootstraps in one dispatch, lazily loads each entity at most once at a time, debounces persistence with text-field flush support, and uses `shallow` selector equality so updates to entity `A` do not force subscribers of entity `B` to re-render.
